name: Chocolate Bars
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Margarine (2 Sticks), at room temperature
Dry substitute equal to 1/3 Cup sugar
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine flour, sugar, cocoa, baking soda, dry sugar substitute, cinnamon, and salt.
2. Mix on low speed to blend well.
3. Add margarine, eggs, vanilla extract, and water; mix on medium speed until fully blended.
4. Spread batter evenly into an 11" x 15" jelly roll pan sprayed with pan spray or greased with margarine.
5. Bake at 325°F for 20-25 minutes, or until bars easily pull away from the sides of the pan and a cake tester inserted in the center comes out clean.
6. Remove from oven and let cool on a wire rack. Sprinkle chocolate chips evenly over the top of the hot bars.
7. Divide into four by eight pieces and allow to cool until chocolate is set.
8. Cut as marked.
Food Exchange per serving: 1 STARCH EXCHANGE + 1 FAT EXCHANGE
CHO: 14g; PRO: 2g; FAT: 7g; CAL: 123
Low-sodium diets: Omit salt. Use salt-free margarine.
Desserts for Diabetic by Mabel Cavaiani, R.D.
Brought to you and yours by Nancy O'Brion and her Meal-Master
